name: Kellyanne Conway
subject: This doesn’t pass the smell test
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
Peter,

When it comes to polling, there's certain organizations you simply can't trust.

I know that better than anyone...

**[As President Trump’s campaign manager in 2016, I saw firsthand just how wildly wrong the mainstream media’s polls were!](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/z3BI3ib-L7wVw67Wxnt2OpEuizGxglB3fa4UMssXwudwSAUE4QaFZ4SSjJkpFVqtxZoDUNDZfl90PwnGloyem1PaM8csk7awR-9lv5u0Vl0ECKmP7rc62cDylH9jbY62R8QPNVANJZ7arLG92tmcNHSQGYm2NDydU9ZUNpFEHYIjHmZSXF1fQSsmn6ohAnyQgfQiyaP_yFGKXmhYIHiip6c0ugNgZx2ZZhoHT9bep0IRGf7-wfaUKphV2zFLoyPGwpV8fAr6vyZlQjtsDnXbQfXKEmkTp5wN-1PjSUkzvyuUM3gMt1VEd2t6uEYjk1-KhPqILRQn7y7m5WfkNSE1FHqEK7lhhFXajOn2ztntmiHXxzA-U4BWG1OE17ZwjnwehTc0fQnEcRkE59k1vkKM-8iUsQ13CTfxajoIOUbfJaFoPfFFMZvCKGCxcvv7ajcPQPYs5tPztVh89VqPc06lJpHyWe7yFtGCY1L5QUJHqJO6NSqiUouM-NTLl9zK_todssZR3cOlbnKXjGFQ_UOf_OlUt_Oo0ANwcR4dAAe7x-UegDHD7z1WcuO9L0YQNH8GgegqjLb-DRlNl5TfIK-uJPoP2Y4LePYS9OblcjIgCXCfJaElgviIM0eYGrq0lmYq2JfeFijq6eAHtvgMc5eM7nQ9yIsTB-I28xpS9p0Wfdv0M8ExZKZ_fgddlt50WlQljxtTudSK4nKDQXGInkDYE4RyLxW02kQo6AwuXCuYDE5TR76NolPdbFaB68pCdkMo3nwHWSQiido9fdSjimJ_UWkphLJnixy4iafRIZkuJkq1AxaNAecTeFbvlcnoIRaj0FqRHCHki6rATyEJbmnw2Njb_B6Bvckj6lp6YbhbSzmqJf9gW0zh2U2otdtzghR-SRG3xd4IK-cyc-ysedK5C2Wi-c2Jfs0-UFdhZz8LF0tZchbyOaVFpTR_xFBgeBeXOBYXwYFdTeDQPsbP-U44UUWKvZB3pWYwhKAiBxGrP-D0dOTWu881U5xO8CF78jXFiJ134r5eMqwPwns/3MIj3obMXjSbPMx4L0ttn7JoDEEi1HQh/18)**

clean_body: Peter, When it comes to polling, there's certain organizations you simply can't trust. I know that better than anyone... That 's why it's so vital that we conduct our own polling, especially when there's a discrepancy with the numbers like what we're seeing right now. On one hand, polling shows voters prefer Republicans over Democrats on nearly every key issue: [Source: Morning Consult] But at the same time, polling by the mainstream media shows Democrats have surged out to a whopping on the generic ballot for the midterms. Something doesn't add up... But the only way to do that is to have EVERY Republican and swing voter verify their voter profile before the upcoming verification deadline. That 's why I'm taking this time to personally email you with a simple request, Peter: ******************************************************************************************************** Thank you, Kellyanne Conway | Paid for by the NRSC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. |

body:
| | ICYMI, Peter: This week in Montana, voters witnessed a brazen attempt to rig the field. Minutes before the filing deadline, Sen. Steve Daines dropped out of his race – just after a Republican ally filed to run – effectively blocking anyone else from entering. Convenient timing, right?

**Marie called out this shady move for what it is: an anti-democratic power play, and exactly the kind of backroom politics people are fed up with. Then, she brought this fight to the national stage.**
| _“People think that they have a right to choose their successor, and that is not what our country is founded on.**It is profoundly disrespectful to the American people** , and it destroys the institution that they're saying they want to choose a successor for. To say that you get to choose your successor, **that's not what people have bled and fought for in this country.** ”_

clean_body: | | ICYMI, Peter: This week in Montana, voters witnessed a brazen attempt to rig the field. Minutes before the filing deadline, Sen. Steve Daines dropped out of his race – just after a Republican ally filed to run – effectively blocking anyone else from entering. Convenient timing, right? Marie called out this shady move for what it is: an anti-democratic power play, and exactly the kind of backroom politics people are fed up with. Then, she brought this fight to the national stage. | “People think that they have a right to choose their successor, and that is not what our country is founded on.It is profoundly disrespectful to the American people , and it destroys the institution that they're saying they want to choose a successor for. To say that you get to choose your successor, that's not what people have bled and fought for in this country. ” = Clip from CNN’s “The Lead with Jake Tapper.” CNN. March 5, 2026. Marie isn’t letting this election rigging go unchecked. She’s pushing back to demand transparency and ensure our leaders are actually accountable to the people they’re supposed to represent. But, speaking out like this makes Marie a top target for those who want to maintain the status quo. We must prove that Marie has what it takes to withstand anything they throw our way. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | = | = | = | = | = | = | | | = | Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is a fifth-generation Washingtonian, a small business owner and a pro-choice mother. As the representative for Washington’s third Congressional District, she's working to bring the voices of working people back to DC. name: LCV Triple Match
subject: Trump plans to hand AI companies their own power plants — and you’re paying the price
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTM1MzY4MzcS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjQyMDA2MDMyMAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
TRIPLE MATCH ACTIVE NOW!Renew your LCV membership for just $15 before March 31 to have your impact matched 3X >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/~ In his State of the Union address, Trump announced a surprise deal with AI
industry executives to continue the explosive growth of data centers that are
raising utility costs and environmental concerns across the country. Let’s be clear about who’s paying the price, Peter: we are. Trump’s backdoor deal with AI companies could let them build and operate their own power plantswith no regard for our environment, public health, or communities they’ll
affect. Data centers are consuming a mind-boggling amount of the country’s electricity.
Their usage is growing every day — while utility bills for everyday people are
skyrocketing. The environmental movement must respond. To help power LCV’s campaigns to STOP Trump’s corporate giveaways, a
group of donors is offering to TRIPLE MATCH every contribution that comes in
before March 31, up to our $25,000 goal. Please, renew today while your impact goes 3X as far to power our work ahead of the November midterms. Right now, every $15=$45 >> https://links.messages.lcv. TRIPLE YOUR IMPACT: RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP >> https://links.messages.lcv. Instead of addressing the serious environmental and financial costs of their
industry, AI companies are counting on yet another backroom deal and corporate
giveaway —they’ll get richer as everyday people struggle to make ends meet. This fall’s midterm elections will determine who controls Congress — and right
now, MAGA Republicans are rushing to lock in as many pro-polluter policies as
possible for their corporate backers before voters get to have their say at the
ballot box. Congressional Republicans may be content to let massive corporations write their
own rules, but it’s up to the voters to decide who runs Congress. Renewing your LCV membership today helps: ✅ Support one of the biggest voter registration efforts in history
✅ Recruit grassroots organizers in states across the country to protect the
right to vote
✅ Oppose MAGA legislators’ attempts to rig the midterm elections in their favor
✅ Defend our climate and our democracy from Trump’s dirty agenda Please, renew for just $15 today to end the Trump administration’s backroom deals. Through March 31, every dollar you give TRIPLES in impact >> https://links.messages.lcv. Thank you for standing with us. — LCV’s Membership Team This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=13536837&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjQyMDA2MDMyMAS2&spReportId=MjQyMDA2MDMyMAS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=13536837&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjQyMDA2MDMyMAS2&spReportId=MjQyMDA2MDMyMAS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

Washington, DC 20005

name: Christopher Ahuja for Congress
subject: Brad Sherman Just Voted to Protect Sexual Predators
body:
Political game-playing doesn't protect victims

| | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=cff73f9834&e=53c7d12fc7)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,
This week, Brad Sherman had a choice. He could have supported transparency for survivors of sexual misconduct in Congress. Instead, he chose a familiar Washington maneuver: bury the issue in process and move on.
The measure, **H.Res. 1072,** would require the release of Ethics Committee records related to sexual misconduct investigations involving Members of Congress, while protecting victims’ identities. The goal is simple: transparency and accountability.
Sherman voted to send it to the Ethics Committee.
**In Washington, that is a classic way to kick the can down the road**. Issues get “reviewed,” “studied,” and quietly buried in bureaucracy while the public never gets answers and survivors never see justice.
There is another uncomfortable truth here. The bill was introduced by Republican Congresswoman Nancy Mace, a polarizing figure many Democrats disagree with.
That should not matter.
If a bill increases transparency about sexual misconduct in Congress while protecting victims, it deserves a vote. Dismissing it because of who introduced it is exactly the kind of partisan game that undermines trust in government.
Worse, it accomplishes nothing for survivors. It delays accountability and hands Republicans an easy talking point about Democrats playing politics.
Survivors deserve better than political games and bureaucratic delays.
I’m running for Congress because protecting institutions should never come before protecting people. Accountability should not depend on party labels.
[**If you agree, chip in today to help us bring real transparency and reform to Washington.**](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=7b45bcc051&e=53c7d12fc7)

---
| | **In solidarity,**[](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=fd79068c35&e=53c7d12fc7)**Chris Ahuja****Democratic Candidate for Congress (CA-32)**

Folks, did you know Colorado is now the third most expensive state to live in?
I hear the same problem from voters all the time: Colorado families are feeling the burden of grocery costs, housing costs, insurance costs, and much more. Meanwhile, paychecks aren’t keeping up with inflation, and too many good jobs are disappearing.
>From Grand Junction to Pueblo, people are working harder than ever and still falling behind. That’s wrong.
I’m running for Congress to bring real opportunity back to CO-03, and my top priority is simple: jobs, jobs, and more jobs. We need leadership that puts affordability front and center. Will you donate to help us build a campaign strong enough to win? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak?&]

Good-paying jobs that will let people build a life here without having to move away, sustain their whole families, strengthen our small businesses, and keep our communities thriving.
This race will determine Colorado’s future work for working people. Let’s fight for an economy that works for everyone. Give to my campaign today » [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak?&]

I know what it’s like to have the deck stacked against you, friend.

Growing up in Dallas, life wasn’t always easy. I was raised by a single mom on a teacher’s salary who sometimes had to work another job to make ends meet. Without her sacrifice and the support of our community, I wouldn’t be where I am today.

I went on to play in the NFL, become a civil rights lawyer, and proudly serve in the Obama administration. I went on to run for office in 2018 and unseat a 22-year GOP incumbent nobody thought I had a chance _in hell_ to beat. I went on to run for Senate and build one of the largest grassroots movements Texas has ever seen.

**Now, I’m ready to work twice as hard to secure victory in our runoff election – but it’s going to take serious resources to reach all the voters we need to win.**

[If you can, please chip in a split donation of $10, $20, or any amount today to help keep up our incredible momentum and power our fight to win in TX-33 and flip the House blue.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/104376?t=12&akid=23200%2E878179%2E_szb4e)

Onward. Thank you.

Colin Allred

name: Melanie V. Ramil
subject: intro: Melanie + you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Hi there — I wanted to take a moment to introduce myself.
My name is Melanie V. Ramil, and I’m honored to step into the role of Executive Director of the California Democratic Party. I’m joining this team at a moment when the stakes for our state and our country could not be higher.
The truth is that California plays an outsized role in the fight for the future of this country. When Democrats organize here, we don’t just win elections in our own communities — we help shape the balance of power in Washington. That’s why the work of the California Democratic Party matters so much right now.
And the work we do is real, grassroots, year-round organizing. We call it Forever Organizing.
We put organizers on the ground in competitive districts who build relationships with voters long before an election year. We train volunteers who are ready to knock on doors and talk to their neighbors about what’s at stake. We invest in voter protection and multilingual voter resources so every Californian has the information they need to participate in our democracy. And so much more.
This model has helped California Democrats deliver critical victories in recent years. Grassroots organizing helped us flip three seats from red to blue in 2024, pass Proposition 50 by a landslide last November, and mobilize millions of voters across California who believe in a more just and inclusive future.
But here’s the honest truth: Organizing at this scale takes resources.
Every canvass we launch requires materials, training, and staff support to make it successful. Every volunteer phonebank and textbank requires the technology and infrastructure that allows thousands of conversations with voters to happen at once. And every organizer we place in a competitive district ensures that Democrats are building community and long-term power — not just showing up a few weeks before Election Day.
That’s why grassroots support is so critical to everything we do.
As I step into this role, my goal is simple: To make sure our organizing program is stronger, more community and people focused, and more prepared than ever for the fights ahead. It’s why I’m here and why I am so thrilled to work with you.
And, all this cannot happen without supporters like you.
So, I’m asking you personally: If you believe in the mission of the California Democratic Party and the work we’re doing to organize communities across the state, will you consider making a grassroots donation today to help power this next chapter? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-march26?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-march26?&refcode=&amount=]I’m grateful to be part of this team and this movement, and I’m excited — and ready — for the work we’re going to do together in the months and years ahead. Let’s do this, team.
With joy + gratitude,
Melanie V. Ramil
Executive Director
California Democratic Party

DONATE » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=&amount=20.24]
Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action.
If this email was forwarded to you, please sign up for our email list here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/zWMh-KAhmUiJYxMzOxKADQ2]
We rely on email to communicate with supporters like you and conduct the business of the California Democratic Party, but if you wish to receive only our most important emails, let us know here to sign up for fewer emails. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/60txJAHUg0OznGGaWVwO_A2]
And if you no longer wish to receive emails from the California Democratic Party, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.cadem.org/p/BZcF64X0Zkie6fv4FEU9PQ2?unsubscribedata=XpLjI%2FsHo4n4nFNbdG8kwNKwMES2At5EYeS9fNkHtmtG1a8kDv1ucnoRn056RXzGdk3e7AlzQ0X60Elg21bL5CNeAWBqqW5qlqW40syRnjlTGdvygTB5yOYgFpKXuS%2Bgn9tykqGPNd4TMh08QE1JbhSIGb72U%2BqDzSh6APgP1e25mUcWcCEBKkh9Z7iC99sTRgoajdZodarYgTuUJP3jj6xEH0N6FpShAgeZ08BP2FuzVGnoNkW6UY60vZ7U5DrWOnDK4g080M%2Fj3phiOxbKtx6ktQkNs3SgWdIjaYZ33ZhpA63AfGG1K%2F29u%2Bn%2BxxR%2B260qBuvxJq3bbX36IzKiCaEPCtrWC%2F%2BZeYcxtZEkPgZMa0I3zoAj%2FyUcX147Ebp5yJvNgY1y6o9yipwfpHUNIxW6Hdy5HmlIp10pb9FjSto%3D .

name: Rory Hoskins
subject: ICE stormed the city where I serve as Mayor
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-crockett-sen [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-crockett-sen]
I want to tell you a story that says more about why I am running for Congress than any slogan or talking point ever could.
Earlier this year, in the middle of the afternoon, ICE agents stormed Concordia Cemetery in Forest Park, where I’m proud to serve as Mayor. The workers on site had just tried to help a man who was struggling in the river. They were doing their jobs. They were watching out for human life. They were on private property.
Then ICE showed up.
Agents cut the lock off the cemetery gate. They pushed their way inside. They fired pepper balls and pepper spray at the workers. They slammed men to the ground. They zip-tied four of them and hauled them away for questioning. And every single one of those workers was a United States citizen.
Let me pause on that. These were working people. Groundskeepers. Fathers. Neighbors. They were not suspects. They were not fugitives. They were not even the individuals ICE claimed to be looking for.
The agents held them for hours. No charges. No explanation. No respect for due process, private property, or basic constitutional rights.
I stood with those workers the next day. I listened as they described what it felt like to be thrown to the ground in a place families consider sacred. I heard how agents tried to intimidate them. I saw the bruises. And I made one thing very clear.
What happened in that cemetery was unlawful, unconstitutional, and unacceptable.
My job as mayor is to protect the people of Forest Park. My job as a human being is to defend dignity wherever it is under attack. So I called on ICE leadership to explain why their agents behaved like an unaccountable militia. I demanded answers. I brought attention to the workers’ story. I made sure they had support and legal assistance. And I refused to let anyone in Washington pretend this was normal.
I am running for Congress because this cannot continue. If federal agents can break into a cemetery in broad daylight and assault four innocent workers without consequences, then we have lost something fundamental. We have lost the principle that no one is above the law. We have lost the idea that government power must be accountable to the people. We have lost the basic promise that the Constitution protects every single one of us.
I am not willing to lose that.
If you believe we need members of Congress who will enforce the Constitution against abuses of power, I am asking you to stand with me. Chip in whatever you can to help me and Resist Reclaim Rebuild take this fight to the halls of Congress. $10, $25, or even $50 would make such a massive difference to this team.
DONATE TO MY CAMPAIGN TODAY! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-rory]
Your support will help us introduce this campaign to voters who have never heard my name, but who care deeply about fairness, safety, and the rule of law.
Thank you for reading this. Thank you for believing in a politics that protects people instead of targeting them. And thank you for standing with me in this fight.
Sincerely,
Rory Hoskins
Democrat for Congress
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-crockett-sen]
Support Rory Hoskins for IL-7! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-rory]

* * *
## Political Satire That Writes Itself!
In the past month alone, AI parody creators have turned the MAGA cinematic universe into a full-blown comedy genre. [Donald Trump has become everything from a rage-tweeting emperor to a Broadway belter, while Melania delivers deadpan monologues that make her a First Lady in moral and mental collapse](https://substack.com/redirect/61854994-5abb-4609-8b57-1d2b3fbe8d01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Not to be outdone, Senator Markwayne Mullin--who as new DHS Secretary is supposed to build a wall to separate the U.S. and Mexico, but couldn't even build a wall between his two first names-- becomes a hyper-macho action hero who somehow manages to wrestle both reality and grammar at the same time. And the fired Kristi Noem is our overzealous superhero--complete with dramatic, nonsensical speeches about law and order that spiral into absurdity.
The results are hilarious, a little surreal, and surprisingly revealing: [when AI exaggerates the personalities of the 5-star idiots, the charlatans, the venal cupcakes and political theatrics already dominating the news cycle, the satire practically writes itself](https://substack.com/redirect/61854994-5abb-4609-8b57-1d2b3fbe8d01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Like this hilarious collection of AI parodies. . \- Cliff | | ---|---|--- Political Satire That Writes Itself! In the past month alone, AI parody creators have turned the MAGA cinematic universe into a full-blown comedy genre. . Not to be outdone, Senator Markwayne Mullin--who as new DHS Secretary is supposed to build a wall to separate the U.S. and Mexico, but couldn't even build a wall between his two first names-- becomes a hyper-macho action hero who somehow manages to wrestle both reality and grammar at the same time. And the fired Kristi Noem is our overzealous superhero--complete with dramatic, nonsensical speeches about law and order that spiral into absurdity. The results are hilarious, a little surreal, and surprisingly revealing: . One more reminder: thank you for reading, commenting, and generally supporting Blue Amp Media! name: The Democratic Party
subject: Our road to taking back Congress:
body:
---
Derek, This is a longer email, but we hope you’ll stick around for an explanation about Democrats’ roadmap to winning back Congress this year. **If you don’t have the time, we’ll cut straight to the chase:** [**Democrats have a real path to flip the House and the Senate this November, but there’s still a lot of work to be done. Can you chip in $7 to the DNC today to help Democrats across the country win in November?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&amount=7&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[CONTRIBUTE $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
Here’s the situation:
**DEMOCRATS ONLY NEED TO FLIP THREE SEATS TO TAKE BACK THE HOUSE.**
House Republicans have proven that they’re unwilling to speak out against Trump’s disastrous agenda. They’ve passed budgets kicking millions of Americans off of their health insurance and food assistance, given a blank check to ICE, and done nothing while Trump worsens the affordability crisis with tariffs. _Flipping just three House seats this year would give Democrats a majority and put an end to Trump’s unrestricted reign of chaos._
**FLIPPING FOUR SEATS WILL GIVE DEMOCRATS THE SENATE MAJORITY.**
Democrats’ path to reclaiming our Senate majority revolves around defending our seats in Georgia, Michigan, and New Hampshire, and flipping Republican-held seats in North Carolina, Ohio, Maine, and Texas. And while the polls show that Democrats have the advantage generally with voters right now, we need to make sure that Democrats are still in the lead when votes are counted in November.
**REPUBLICANS ARE TRYING THEIR HARDEST TO STOP US.**
Trump, his Republican allies, and their billionaire funders know that a Democratic victory in November would bring their destructive agenda to a grinding halt, so they’ve been working tirelessly to rig the playing field.
Trump has threatened to nationalize running elections. He’s threatened states for access to their voter rolls to force voter purges. He and his allies even entered 2026 with a _$483 million_ war chest at their disposal, and will undoubtedly attempt to BUY Republican victories this year.
**Derek, it’s going to be an uphill battle, but one that we can win if we all come together to do our part.**
[**Today’s the day we’re asking you to do just that. Can you make a $7 donation directly to the DNC to fund our work, and help us win back the House and the Senate this year?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&amount=7&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._
| | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?express_lane=true&amount=250&refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2026?refcode=em_20260306_6299_fr&refcode2=6299_22930333_Z7wwn4&akid=6299%2E22930333%2EZ7wwn4)**
---
****
We appreciate your support,
The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

# Cleared Field and Increasing Momentum: Magsig Remains Lone Republican in SD12 Race as Filing Period Ends
---
|
---
| | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** March 6th, 2026Contact: nathan@nathanmagsig.com
---
| | | | | | **Supervisor Nathan Magsig Thanks Supporters for Successful Fundraiser, Announces Uncontested Republican Field**

**(Clovis, CA)** \- Fresno County Supervisor **Nathan Magsig** issued the following statement today thanking supporters for attending a successful fundraising event last night in Clovis and acknowledging the close of the candidate filing period for the upcoming State Senate District 12 election.
“Last night’s event was an incredible show of support from friends, community leaders, and residents from across the Central Valley,” said Magsig. “I’m grateful to everyone who came out, contributed, and continues to believe in the work we’re doing to keep our communities strong. I am especially thankful for Senate Republican Leader Brian Jones attending.”
Magsig noted that the funds raised will not only support his ongoing efforts to serve residents of Senate District 12, but will also help support other Republican candidates - Guillermo Gonzales (SD16) and Darin Dupont (SD14) - who share the commitment to strong communities, responsible government, and economic opportunity in the Central Valley.
“With the filing period now officially closed, I’m honored to be the lone Republican candidate in my race,” Magsig added. “While I’m grateful for the confidence our community has shown, I remain focused on the work ahead, supporting public safety, strengthening our local economy, and investing in Guillermo Gonzales and Darin Dupont’s campaigns so we can finally restore common sense in Sacramento.”
Magsig emphasized that his priority remains delivering results for the families, farmers, and small businesses that make the Valley such a special place. In addition to supporting Republican efforts to flip Senate seats in the Valley.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | ###
Nathan Magsig is a current Fresno County Supervisor, former Mayor of Clovis, and is running for the 12th Senate District. His family, wife Julie and their three children, reside in Clovis, CA.

body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40946%2E3422442%2EumepSH)
Peter,
Brand new polling shows **Katie Porter** as the top Democrat in this race.
That’s huge. It means voters are responding to her record of taking on corporate greed, grilling CEOs, and fighting for working families. It means Californians want a fighter.
But here’s the problem.
**Billionaire Tom Steyer just dumped another 28 million dollars of his own money into this race.**
We’ve seen this playbook before. Billionaires and corporate backed candidates flood the airwaves. They drown out grassroots voices. They try to buy their way to winning. **We can’t let this happen.**
If Katie is going to make it through June’s primary, it will be because regular people step up. Not corporate PACs. Not billionaire self-funding. People like you, Peter.
Remember, because of California’s ‘jungle primary’ system, the top two candidates, regardless of political party, will advance to the general election in November.
Your donation today will help us reach the voters we need to win on June 2nd. This is a people-powered campaign. And right now, we need you.
**[Can you rush a donation to help Katie hold her lead and make it into the top two? Let’s show that California can’t be bought. Chip in $5 now.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260306_1_ak.TbVnwP&refcode=em20260306-40946&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40946%2E3422442%2EumepSH)**

Team Porter

name: Adam Schwarze
subject: She regrets her vote. I never will.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
Chip in $2 today to send a SEAL who stands firm—no regrets. | [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=dca483ba4e114e97a847702b099d9cbf&_e=tbHhkO1BaZs-bhoWc2iweZaBNbuljbAz5fNZ71rnxqDfDiyTJ9PNwbWmQpVrdF05nrf8IbN9HSrKVs9uPcIpUnfg5tj5J-b8fJM2BzMOSr6iMWj2l_H4zRpKCtbJGMR3lS86lJ7BnP3Dfi1dJIC-oVbj4BuW3Uh4aBeoAsjX_5HDDmOkjqEQwuCVzJ2mo3Nu1BNU4m6Tndwb1GA3zMZpekh5rUDFmdphH0JCcEfSqdtZABC0QIiALXaRev-bIKZ6YASKiBZb0j5C46R6DdvQttmrK-d2zLptf2qiTstFhwElFUgo7ET9KzIeWrrsui-Eg6ED-_1AYgFA3REPVmYWrwfReG-WWRDyN9_ulKEm0aT8Jmsqov00IiUSibRzn5e6DICFssgPIxxJYo29v50lnyUm4D30IDlFagjlvr9Sx_k0j30RmbCJSkHhkfrfMBNNdXKroTeXfOpNY0MWArDw6w_jCfbUyKGgxmFGzzPswehB_2yiJ_s5xx6z2-O0XVx47Jf3y3QfopjEzzYHBS55-kb5TOZTMjA5Zfmg27WUzn9mm7HaXho8ouE-CkShGVYE1J2Us9YcdmWZoKYGu-zp5eUl9O-lTj5a270RVjg_k9ya-AvAsuf-5xWHLchDdBWZ0qwHpwMIUuN0KLD1YtpjvyuJYcUrjp222DzSECvlrP4Mb2mrUMlpAQhcKtM_NFdIUHc9yayC7kYMOwQ4tFydqODdbeoytuNZYbbXXaP74uI%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

I'm Adam Schwarze, current Republican U.S. Senate candidate in Minnesota, a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and a Minnesotan who deployed nine times to keep threats far from our shores—so families like yours could live without fear.

As a SEAL, I learned early: You don't second-guess the mission once it's underway. You commit, you execute, you finish—no retreat, no regrets.

That's why Rep. Angie Craig's (a Democrat opponent in the race) latest flip-flop hits so hard.

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Our next primary fights are approaching on March 17th
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

So far, Justice Democrats is 1 for 2 in the 2026 Democratic primary
fights.

This past Tuesday, we successfully filled the open TX-30 House seat with
progressive pastor Rev. Frederick Haynes III. In the NC-04 rematch between
Justice Democrat Nida Allam and a corporate-backed incumbent, we came up
short by just 1,202 votes after AI super PACs flooded the race with almost
$2 million in ads.

There are still 10 more opportunities for us to deliver defeat to AIPAC,
Big Tech, and the corporate Democratic establishment, and elect champions
for the working-class — and the next two are rapidly approaching in less
than 2 weeks.

On Tuesday, March 17th, we can elect progressive leaders Junaid Ahmed and
Kat Abughazaleh to fill two open House seats in Illinois — but AIPAC is
once again spending everything they can to stop us.

Through shady super PACs United Democracy Project, Elect Chicago Women,
Chicago Progressive Partnership, and Affordable Chicago, AIPAC has
funnelled almost $15 million into Illinois congressional races — including
IL-08 and IL-09 — in just the last 30 days.

They’re desperate to send two status quo Democrats to represent these
districts in Congress so they can maintain unconditional U.S. military aid
for Israel’s genocide and ethnic cleansing AND back Trump’s war with Iran
like AIPAC is demanding.

Junaid and Kat are both anti-AIPAC candidates who will unequivocally fight
for the sovereignty and human rights of Palestinians while opposing more
forever wars like Trump and Netanyahu’s reckless assault on Iran.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260306-1.AQsw2-&refcode=em20260306-37536&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37536%2E1203867%2EGiLDxL ]Please, pitch in $5 or anything you can to fuel our work
in these final 9 days, beat AIPAC, and send Junaid and Kat to join the
rest of our bloc in Congress!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260306-1.AQsw2-&refcode=em20260306-37536&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1021&akid=37536%2E1203867%2EGiLDxL ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260306-37536-footer&t=1022&refcode2=37536_1203867_GiLDxL&akid=37536%2E1203867%2EGiLDxL

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1023&akid=37536%2E1203867%2EGiLDxL

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

Friend,

Most versions of the memo were [**deleted.**](https://links.rogersforsenate.com/u/click?_t=293de5a6223e4436ab53fb8020578890&_m=95c0d89cb67441c48dd394a6f968a321&_e=jmQjcWWpVhzxeyEFXen5sm5fw4X5cyduDTYTnAuw-s8MVinAYzKhRgckCDDA2duAMgne1DpoegWBEGqLuVN8FSKTJwJZ9Bolj8iSPO50zibR39ZgWxVa0Cx67fbfgNCCJuv9sYHyvTSFZ37BiVDisqVKpdmDjZtuGqtRQZk3XZMorSF_4_AH42tO3GfS8st4YHKi4XyJ6Wb_sZSFu1bimGJdUvdMLBHY_y_xzh2rFheftTBusVOz4wDV1_JbooM991wjfsyRkXAMaSnQ3v-u6iuJuKJa3Y5GHOZzrV1H-zAjiaZ4cWehukrvKI6qem54MfwovtlaJo4l9dHe4kYoZtWIIZixMb-S-tkBAIvNqhZ_CybU0ahMjQuRCWRjMBkAjZE-7A2fywzPUMr0y4CXielfERGZHM5FFbsZzfKY4y83LkPM5ANl8biZA2ce0RrZqwcR1XPv4DaMy49iQIaTWMqlK8eio6_b7kwcd_94G5sRE5bpwZZMD5AT8FCOrZ9HafGzzF8EEmA9NZfAiV2W7oygI_2ularOY-NwueuelCObsGtsn5rHQ7nU64irPVu6B_01H04sMrqUEmkD1XFyaif2I64hTRkMuvwvSeJ5MtgGRxr4cvZBSg6kQwghtJ0hxao_LuuvLHiO650-wF71Q0LzgyBEMWyoMWhpNqJ6Eg_sjBWpMNUZlQpOLHSFA6hfyLft8Xs-hNSaLMQgs5kvXF3Df8HXU5tTA__tRXx_JUTuPGXMGElvdJcNCK9Ol1Nxk3ktm4PWe6fvDi1G_e8dGw%3D%3D)

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Judicial Watch Sues for Gain-of-Function Records
body:
Public Schools Use Federal Violence Prevention Grants for Illegal
Immigrants

[INSIDE JW]

HEARING SET IN JUDICIAL WATCH LAWSUIT ON NIH ROYALTY PAYMENTS

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37292&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj0ySnFSS3JnanZ4NCZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=acad03e5793fba7c1a355ae3b4928253ffde342044d228d6aacd1ea06202c70f]

We have sued the government to expose royalties paid to government
scientists for their inventions.

Now, a federal court has scheduled an evidentiary hearing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37281&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtaGhzLW5paC1yb3lhbHRpZXMtaGVhcmluZy0wMjgyMS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=c5b442ac434a09c2bfcac349ad80fd756ac2f682cc4d9d1976680f0d6530ebcb]
in our Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit filed on behalf of
Open The Books. The lawsuit seeks records of royalty payments made to
scientists and employees at the National Institutes of Health (NIH).

It specifically asks for records concerning former National Institute
of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID) Director Dr. Anthony Fauci,
whose institute participated extensively in the NIH royalty program.

The hearing will take place March 10, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET before
U.S. District Court Judge Amit P. Mehta in the U.S. District Court for
the District of Columbia in the case _American Transparency
(OpenTheBooks.com) v. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=26166&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL29wZW50aGVib29rcy1jb20tdi1oaHMtZmF1Y2ktY29tcGxhaW50LTAyODIxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b33e79cf6248521f2a0702265ea478b4300d17761ab61e8bb11513d11bc49076]
(No. 1:21-cv-02821).

The hearing will address a key dispute over whether the NIH improperly
withheld records showing how much individual government scientists
received through royalty payments tied to taxpayer-funded inventions.

Mehta ordered the hearing after finding a “genuine dispute of
material fact” regarding the NIH’s claim that releasing the
royalty payments made to individual employees would reveal
confidential commercial information from third parties licensing
government patents. The NIH argues that outsiders could allegedly
“back-calculate” confidential royalty rates and payments made by
licensees if the amounts paid to government inventors were disclosed.

Judicial Watch and Open The Books dispute that claim and argue that
disclosing the amounts
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37282&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cub3BlbnRoZWJvb2tzLmNvbS9uaWgtc2VjcmV0LXRoaXJkLXBhcnR5LXJveWFsdHktZGF0YWJhc2UtdW5jb3ZlcmVkLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=0d844f7d4cbb10a1fa3cd9035a729f0cc83d22850ba5cd79e918e47375bc04a3]
paid to government employees does not reveal confidential commercial
information and is required under the Freedom of Information Act. We
contend that the NIH’s theory is speculative and improperly used to
conceal how much taxpayer-funded government employees received in
royalty income.

In earlier rulings in the case, Mehta rejected the NIH’s effort to
broadly shield the royalty program using employee privacy claims,
writing that “federal government employees have a limited privacy
interest in information concerning their compensation.”

The court also emphasized the strong public interest in disclosure,
noting that transparency regarding royalty payments could help the
public assess whether inventors’ financial interests in licensed
technologies “could potentially bias the design, conduct, or
reporting of clinical research.” Mehta further concluded that the
public interest in understanding these financial arrangements is
significant, particularly where government scientists involved in
taxpayer-funded biomedical research may receive payments tied to the
commercialization of those technologies.

The October 2021 lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=26601&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctc3Vlcy1uaWgtcm95YWx0aWVzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=c52c6e8dc5a6d923b4df3e7b07343b6b422737f9c9fe82ce237e3b1475901c5e] was filed after
the NIH failed to adequately respond to FOIA requests seeking records
about royalty payments and financial arrangements involving NIH
personnel.

The FOIA requests sought records including:

* Employment contracts for NIH employees
* Financial disclosure and conflict-of-interest records
* Confidentiality agreements
* Job descriptions for senior officials, including Dr. Anthony Fauci
* Records regarding royalty payments received by NIH scientists and
employees

Over $2.685 billion
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37283&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cub3BlbnRoZWJvb2tzLmNvbS9hc3NldHMvMS82L05JSF9Db3JydXB0aW9uX2FuZF9Db25mbGljdF9GSU5BTC5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=580734367868395df71ecc356fc0694a1b1447bc6378b2891bc739f75b329938]
was paid to NIH institutes or scientists – of which more than $1
billion was marked for inventors – between 2010-2023 from
pharmaceutical companies and other private entities licensing
government-owned patents. Those payments were obtained only after we
and Open The Books forced the NIH to release previously hidden royalty
payment records through FOIA litigation.

The disclosures include royalty payments connected to inventions
developed across multiple NIH institutes, including the National
Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID), which was led
for decades by Fauci and played a central role in federally funded
biomedical research.

This upcoming hearing will determine whether the NIH can continue
withholding records showing how much individual government scientists
received in royalty payments. Judicial Watch and Open The Books
already forced disclosure of more than $1 billion of dollars in NIH
royalty payments marked to inventors, like Fauci. The court will now
examine whether the agency can keep secret key details about the
payment amounts tied to those taxpayer-funded inventions.

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37284&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2FtZXJpY2FuLXRyYW5zcGFyZW5jeS12LWhocy1uaWgtcm95YWx0aWVzLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDQzMi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=eff94fdb9188200ec2e6ea1be41ab31a6057bde437d2da81e4de089d223a7d46]
on behalf of Open The Books seeking records concerning whether
statutory limits on royalty payments to federal employees are being
effectively bypassed (_American Transparency v. U.S. Department of
Health and Human Services_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37285&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2FtZXJpY2FuLXRyYW5zcGFyZW5jeS12LWhocy1uaWgtcm95YWx0aWVzLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDQzMi9dXz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=0345c48be82ae105e8a05c3575c0c44399e507b01c973cb7a11f21f559a8e0f6 _(No.
1:26-cv-00432)).

The new lawsuit seeks records including:

* Emails referencing royalty payments that may exceed the statutory
cap
* Records concerning royalty payments placed in reserve when payments
exceed statutory limits
* Internal guidance and procedures governing the NIH Public Health
Service Technology Transfer Policy Manual

The records requested cover the period January 2020 through December
2025.

Federal law limits the amount individual government employee-inventors
may receive in royalty payments to $150,000 per year. NIH scientists
may receive royalty payments when inventions developed with taxpayer
funding are licensed to private companies. These payments originate
from license fees paid by pharmaceutical companies and other entities
seeking to commercialize government-developed biomedical technologies.

The disclosures already forced through our litigation have raised
questions about whether government scientists receiving royalties may
have financial interests connected to technologies or products they
help develop, test, or promote using taxpayer funds.

Transparency, maintains one of the largest independent databases of
public-sector spending in the United States, promoting transparency by
putting government spending records online for public review.

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES DEFENSE DEPARTMENT FOR GAIN-OF-FUNCTION RECORDS

Gain-of-function proposals were likely active in the Defense
Department’s pipeline prior to the pandemic. We’re suing to find
out what these proposals entail.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37256&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9kLWRhcnBhLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDQzMy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=40f25ea3fa8af88e9f48b5d20bc63dfc6f64c690ef716d3243d277434fd890be]
against the U.S. Department of Defense for records on funding
proposals submitted to the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency
(DARPA) Biological Technologies Office prior to the Covid-19 outbreak
(_Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Defense_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37293&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9kLWRhcnBhLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDQzMy9dXz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=2a18becca2feea7bbf41ad2673e0fe6bc35d73063a75235d7011a0247a04aef4 _(No.
1:26-cv-00433)).

Judicial Watch sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of
Columbia after the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, a
component of the Defense (War) Department, failed to respond to a
November 7, 2025, FOIA request for:

> All biodefense and gain-of-function (GoF) funding proposals
> submitted to the DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO) prior to
> December 2019.

The Biological Technologies Office was launched
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37257&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGFycGEubWlsL25ld3MvMjAxNC9iaW9sb2dpY2FsLXRlY2hub2xvZ2llcy1vZmZpY2UtbGF1bmNoP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=110b2450f7c2c3b1403dc194a99ee4b751102a4065dea7abaf12e28c0cc9952f]
in 2014 with DARPA, claiming the office’s programs “push the
leading edge of science” and “will sometimes be society’s first
encounter with the ethical, legal, or social dilemmas that can be
raised by new biological technologies.”

The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee in
April 2024 announced that it sent letters
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37258&F=T&&hash=5f75fcbe2355fddb81be81fe9d66fbc9441c6956dd2b8ad0d8b64db3db0f08a7]
to several agencies requesting:

> [I]nformation on their knowledge and involvement regarding the
> DEFUSE project, a 2018 grant proposal led by EcoHealth Alliance and
> the Wuhan Institute of Virology. The DEFUSE project was submitted to
> the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) under the
> PREventing Emerging Pathogenic Threats (PREEMPT) program and
> proposed to insert a furin cleavage site into a coronavirus to
> create a novel chimeric virus that would have been shockingly
> similar to the COVID-19 virus.

Senator Rand Paul (R-KY), then the ranking member of the committee
wrote
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37259&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZm94bmV3cy5jb20vb3Bpbmlvbi9ncmVhdC1jb3ZpZC1jb3Zlci11cC1zaG9ja2luZy10cnV0aC1hYm91dC13dWhhbi0xNS1mZWRlcmFsLWFnZW5jaWVzXTo/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=a19d165e102fc86ba20c7f9a78a948ce953119d53a7ddff95a05dd1c5aba0956
“[A]t least 15 federal agencies knew from the beginning of the
pandemic that EcoHealth Alliance and the Wuhan Institute of Virology
were seeking federal funding in 2018 to create a virus genetically
very similar if not identical to COVID-19.”

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES TREASURY ON COVERING LARRY SUMMERS’ PORTRAIT

A source inside the Treasury Department informed a Judicial Watch
lawyer that the official portrait of former Treasury Secretary
Lawrence Summers had been covered, reportedly after his name surfaced
in records connected to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

That’s a curious response, and we want to know more.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37272&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdHJlYXN1cnktc2VjLXN1bW1lcnMtY29tcGxhaW50LTAwNDQ2L11hZ2FpbnN0P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=8ceb7e4c33914ac14a76f5c8151ce4625aedbd069f601b95f6501c754e2bdc23
the U.S. Department of Treasury for records regarding the covering
portrait in the Main Treasury Building in Washington, D.C., (_Judicial
Watch v. U.S. Department of Treasury_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37273&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdHJlYXN1cnktc2VjLXN1bW1lcnMtY29tcGxhaW50LTAwNDQ2L11fP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=017a6cd7107d1a9303b1ce42888fbf510c918c377c0d042d1319ce7644928f34 _(No.1:26-cv-00446)).

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the Treasury Department failed to respond to a January 8, 2026, FOIA
request for:

> All records, including communications, about the covering up of
> former Treasury Secretary Lawrence H. Summers’ portrait in the
> U.S. Treasury Building in Washington, D.C. Mr. Summers served as
> Treasury Secretary from July 2, 1999, to January 20, 2001.

In November 2025, soon after his name arose in connection with
Epstein, Summers said
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37274&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9hYmNuZXdzLmNvbS9VUy9kZWVwbHktYXNoYW1lZC1sYXJyeS1zdW1tZXJzLXN0ZXBzLXB1YmxpYy1leWUtYWZ0ZXIvc3Rvcnk/aWQ9MTI3NjE2OTIzJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=2d815a9e2ba70e94fcfa3d332398e275b71b805396102a3fec0d6d4439e9d5da],
“I am deeply ashamed of my actions and recognize the pain they have
caused.... I will be stepping back from public commitments as one part
of my broader effort to rebuild trust and repair relationships with
the people closest to me.”

An exchange of emails
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37275&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZm94bmV3cy5jb20vdXMvbGFycnktc3VtbWVycy1zdGVwcy1wdWJsaWMtcm9sZXMtaG91c2UtcmVsZWFzZS1lcHN0ZWluLWNvcnJlc3BvbmRlbmNlP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=55516c23e6ed054bd9a2c6e8c0eeb420666d483b8963b1815e8b67773fd24b00]
between Summers and Epstein contained in a trove of Epstein records
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37276&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9vdmVyc2lnaHQuaG91c2UuZ292L3JlbGVhc2Uvb3ZlcnNpZ2h0LWNvbW1pdHRlZS1yZWxlYXNlcy1hZGRpdGlvbmFsLWVwc3RlaW4tZXN0YXRlLWRvY3VtZW50cy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=08db84d3f0bc1969586d72c41494be55bc0b41438343acd2c671f0f056459f91]
released by the House Oversight Committee, Epstein called himself
Summers’ “wing man.”

Covering Larry Summers’ portrait won’t make the Epstein scandal
disappear. Covering the portrait raises obvious questions.
Transparency—not cover-ups—is the proper response.

We have been actively pursuing details of the Epstein case.

In November 2025, we filed a lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37277&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtc3Vlcy1kb2otZm9yLWVwc3RlaW4tcmVsYXRlZC1yZWNvcmRzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=bc536690a43abd1a31fb658e8535c4f452c62c81630c7c961bc058e625ea8774]
against the Justice Department for records subpoenaed by the U.S.
House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform regarding accused
sex trafficker Epstein.

In October 2025, we filed a FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37278&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY2lhLWplZmZyZXktZXBzdGVpbi1yZWNvcmRzP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=42bf0aba646d89a2eb83cdcac934e3129fa289e4de9864d760d8d99166709688] against
the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) for records involving any role
Epstein might have played in connection with the agency, his business
dealings, travel, victim or witness information, and records
concerning his death.

In September 2025, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=36089&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9qLWNvbW11bmljYXRpb25zLXdpdGgtYXhpb3MvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=2aa6ae8546034db5938f9b71ca8b7c3b2292a55b711c36adfdda744baf68f84d] the
Justice Department for communications the department had with the
media outlet Axios regarding the outlet’s “exclusives” on the
release of audio of then-President Joe Biden’s August 2023 interview
with then-Special Counsel Robert Hur regarding Biden’s handling of
secret documents and the July 2025 memo that stated there is no

In a Judicial Watch FOIA lawsuit, a “joint status report
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=36624&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZXBzdGVpbi1yZWNvcmRzLXBvc3NpYmxlLXJlbGVhc2UvXeKAnT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=06f8be2d2be0735f1937774103edf4f52960cd31d1a122db3108b16088190e89
filed on July 7, 2025, in federal court reported that the Justice

records in response to our FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=34969&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZXBzdGVpbi1jbGllbnQtbGlzdHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=34eda6c9d9b0ef21bf1e05bfa61d1548434959d0905106eadc7bba0a77797d23] for records

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=35616&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3Yvb3BhL21lZGlhLzE0MDcwMDEvZGw/aW5saW5lJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=805564b89b9de3b63a7154eca9cf68f7c0d73088102489da1dec1cdd26b511c3] that suggests no

PUBLIC SCHOOLS USE FEDERAL VIOLENCE PREVENTION GRANTS FOR ILLEGAL
IMMIGRANTS

Tax dollars aimed at preventing violence are being used instead for
the benefit of illegal immigrants, our _Corruption Chronicles _blog
reports

> A multi-million dollar federal program launched to prevent school
> violence after 17 students and staff were shot dead at a south
> Florida high school is instead being used to fund special projects
> to help overwhelmed campuses deal with the onslaught of illegal
> immigrants. Known as Student Teachers and Officers Preventing (STOP)
> school violence, the Department of Justice (DOJ) created the safety
> plan after the Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in
> Parkland Florida on Feb. 14, 2018. Through its Bureau of Justice
> Assistance (BJA) the DOJ dedicates millions of dollars annually to
> STOP to make schools around the country safer. The goal is “to
> improve school security by providing students and teachers with the
> tools they need to recognize, respond quickly to, and prevent acts
> of violence,” according to the DOJ’s official description
> [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37294&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9iamEub2pwLmdvdi9wcm9ncmFtL3N0b3Atc2Nob29sLXZpb2xlbmNlLXByb2dyYW0vb3ZlcnZpZXc/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=98c473f8a30755e47958c9db5d9ac49fc9d046d9c4a635986487ef47db724b03],
> which further states that the grants are disbursed “to improve
> security within our Nation’s schools and on school grounds through
> evidence-based programs.”

> Instead, over a dozen STOP grants worth $13.5 million have been used
> to fund programs that “explicitly aim to serve foreign students or
> their families in some way,” according to a report
> [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37295&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVudGhlYm9va3Muc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL3Avc2Nob29sLXN5c3RlbS1mZWRlcmFsLWdyYW50ZWVzLXNheT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=d873d721848a7fbc4a9caf5cd2aa5eaed1479858d29297fad5f74107542db196]
> issued recently by a Florida-based nonprofit that runs a vast
> database of public spending for all to see. The group, Open the
> Books, lists 15 STOP grants that were wrongfully spent to

> [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37296&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY29uZ3Jlc3MuZ292L2JpbGwvMTE1dGgtY29uZ3Jlc3MvaG91c2UtYmlsbC80OTA5P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=9174f981c6e3b07604e8bdad10988901d0cc80b5a8d33ad83186e8eff770581d] that
> led to the safety program’s creation. It involves a $1 million
> grant intended to stop violence in the public school district of
> Monroe County Florida diverted to offset the cost of illegal
> immigration. “Monroe County has experienced an influx of migrant

> grant announcement
> [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=80065&L=37297&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudXNhc3BlbmRpbmcuZ292L2F3YXJkL0FTU1RfTk9OXzE1UEJKQTI0R0cwNDM1NVNUT1BfMDE1Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=1edf97054ec35d604551a141610b38bd809d616f97bf83263d6526f800eca06c]
> states. “This has overwhelmed local resources such as law
> enforcement, community mental health, and schools. There has been a
> steady increase in community crimes, and in the last two years there
> has been a 119% increase in school arrests. Immigrant youth
> represent high percentages of offenders, children’s shelter
> occupants, and students with disciplinary actions.”

> The Monroe County grant announcement goes on to explain that
> immigrants coming into the area have been exposed to high levels of
> trauma then are met with high cost of living, lack of housing and no
> community resources. “Youth then have to navigate an educational
> system that is foreign to them while not having the English skills

> factors for juvenile delinquency,” the document states, adding
> that “it also creates a system where teachers feel ill equipped to
> meet the educational, behavioral, and mental health needs of our
> immigrant students, thus leading to low perceptions of school
> climate.” Therefore, the security grant will be used for a program
> called Project Wings that will welcome illegal immigrants and
> nurture their growth and success by providing a four-week trauma
> informed orientation for students who have “experienced community,
> gang, and/or familial violence in their home countries, and/or
> violence during their journey to the United States.” The school
> climate will also be improved for the illegal aliens with a
> trauma-informed cultural awareness training for teachers, activities
> for English language learners and the hiring of two certified
> teachers/counselors to provide social-emotional English language
> immersion as well as small group and individual therapy for students
> and the development of a community-based Spanish and Haitian Creole
> parent-school liaison program.

> The other wrongfully spent STOP grants documented in the report
> include a $1 million allocation to the Sodus Central School District
> in New York for an initiative called “Place to Belong” that
> focuses on an “increasing number of English language learners”
> in the area. Another million-dollar STOP grant went to seven schools
> in Oxnard, California with the “highest numbers of suspensions due
> to incidents of violence with and without injury, socioeconomically
> disadvantaged students, and English language learners.” Miami-Dade
> Public Schools in south Florida, where one-quarter of the student
> population does not speak English, also received a $1 million STOP
> grant that instead was used to accommodate its huge immigrant
> population. “Meeting the needs of such a richly diverse student
> body is a challenging task that has become even more difficult
> post-pandemic,” the district writes to justify redirecting funds
> that Congress specifically intended to improve campus security.
