body:
Govt officials send chilling warning
Please read the message below from our sponsor.
* * *
**The Invisible Threat In Millions of Homes** [](https://trk.rightwingreport.com/?xtl=27asgvk2ds1gybn8cyayb1ev8sidwn0ksar689rjkywx6knti3s9sbahoskkumd38lgd8dur5g5ybkpf0820ii1125ha8hwd1msyf6dcxtt70e2k4px5p52ufvnmp74vw43jbh5rvwk41qcmj6r9ihb672tu5t5rri1vz3yypr9r88ttzgit9o55lypdrd4afgixsbmvn9bjfsmxkqua4e2kf7krs9x8clwwndh9tb8k1bvyqm0skdmmgxbu&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=tbjdvl&__onlt=h) **The worst drinking water outbreak in U.S. history sent 403,000 to the hospital and killed 69. No alarms. No warnings.** The drinking water in their homes appeared safe, but was contaminated with a deadly pathogen. Families didn't fail. The systems they trusted failed. This outbreak proved that clear, normal-looking water can be dangerously contaminated. That's why experts still study this outbreak for one reason: It can happen again. In fact, here's what should truly concern you... Federal authorities and national security experts are now reporting something even more disturbing: **The FBI warns the next deadly contamination may be _intentional_.** _Hackers are attacking water systems in our home towns, overriding safety protocols, weaponizing our utilities against us and positioning themselves to strike when the signal comes._ America's enemies have found a way to strike without firing a single shot, and national security experts warn it's not just China. Iran, Russia and North Korea are also preparing coordinated attacks. Overflowing, damaging and poisoning the lifegiving systems we trust to keep us safe. **Like it or not, your local tap water may be our country's weakest link.** Deteriorating infrastructure. Systems years beyond their expiration dates. Negligent corporations. And God-knows-what in the water we drink. We're up against arsenic, plastics, chlorine, forever chemicals, hormone-altering drug residue and a host of other contaminants. Listen, I'm not trying to be the messenger of doom... _**But protecting the water supply in your home is more important than ever.**_ If you don't take steps to be prepared, you and your family could end up vulnerable. Forced to choose between drinking poisoned, toxic or dirty water, or going without water altogether. It sounds harsh... But the truth is that even good people with good intentions can make serious mistakes. Mistakes like...
* **Getting filters that claim to purify your water, but don't prove it.**
* If a company doesn't have certifications from a third-party lab, show lab tests or reveal how many gallons were tested... that's a bad sign. After a couple gallons, most water filters on the market stop working for scary contaminants and start passing them into your water.
* **Getting bottled water with a 2-year shelf life and risking plastic leaching into your supply.**
* Not to mention all the money you'll waste, and the work required to carry cases of water. Plus with bottled water, you never really know what's been filtered out.
* **Or simply using a pitcher in the fridge... with slapshot filtration power and limited capacity.**
* Most pitchers hold a couple cups of water at a time. Not nearly enough to drink, cook and clean in an emergency. You'll run out in no time, just when your life depends on it.

clean_body: Govt officials send chilling warning Please read the message below from our sponsor. The Invisible Threat In Millions of Homes The worst drinking water outbreak in U.S. history sent 403,000 to the hospital and killed 69. No alarms. No warnings. The drinking water in their homes appeared safe, but was contaminated with a deadly pathogen. Families didn't fail. The systems they trusted failed. This outbreak proved that clear, normal-looking water can be dangerously contaminated. That's why experts still study this outbreak for one reason: It can happen again. In fact, here's what should truly concern you... Federal authorities and national security experts are now reporting something even more disturbing: The FBI warns the next deadly contamination may be intentional. Hackers are attacking water systems in our home towns, overriding safety protocols, weaponizing our utilities against us and positioning themselves to strike when the signal comes. America's enemies have found a way to strike without firing a single shot, and national security experts warn it's not just China. Iran, Russia and North Korea are also preparing coordinated attacks. Overflowing, damaging and poisoning the lifegiving systems we trust to keep us safe. Like it or not, your local tap water may be our country's weakest link. Deteriorating infrastructure. Systems years beyond their expiration dates. Negligent corporations. And God-knows-what in the water we drink. We're up against arsenic, plastics, chlorine, forever chemicals, hormone-altering drug residue and a host of other contaminants. Listen, I'm not trying to be the messenger of doom... But protecting the water supply in your home is more important than ever. If you don't take steps to be prepared, you and your family could end up vulnerable. Forced to choose between drinking poisoned, toxic or dirty water, or going without water altogether. It sounds harsh... But the truth is that even good people with good intentions can make serious mistakes. Mistakes like... Getting filters that claim to purify your water, but don't prove it. If a company doesn't have certifications from a third-party lab, show lab tests or reveal how many gallons were tested... that's a bad sign. After a couple gallons, most water filters on the market stop working for scary contaminants and start passing them into your water. Getting bottled water with a 2-year shelf life and risking plastic leaching into your supply. Not to mention all the money you'll waste, and the work required to carry cases of water. Plus with bottled water, you never really know what's been filtered out. Or simply using a pitcher in the fridge... with slapshot filtration power and limited capacity. name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: We’re making life more affordable. Here’s how:
body:
|
#
Friend, I keep hearing the same thing from folks: everything costs more, and paychecks just aren’t keeping up. All across our district and around the country, hardworking families are being forced to make impossible choices – between buying their medicine, affording to buy meat for a weekend meal, or paying the gas bill. No one should have to live like that, especially families who are working hard and doing everything right. **That’s why my colleagues and I wrote an Affordability Agenda focused on bringing down the everyday costs families feel the most – from groceries and health care to housing, child care, and energy.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126859307/606179144/-1606559032?refcode=em-3.7.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MThiZmNlYS0zZTFhLWYxMTEtYTY5YS0wMDBkM2ExZjBhOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFZjJyQZycsTBBIy7eFRks4_Gj_DMI2pCjCekCiv0DI=) Our plan would expand the $35 insulin cap and the $2,100 annual limit on prescription drug costs so more Americans can benefit. It extends Affordable Care Act tax credits to get premiums down and cracks down on price gouging and Wall Street greed. **This is about making sure families who work hard can actually get ahead – not just scrape by.** But the same special interests driving costs up are already spending big to flip this seat. clean_body: | Friend, I keep hearing the same thing from folks: everything costs more, and paychecks just aren’t keeping up. All across our district and around the country, hardworking families are being forced to make impossible choices – between buying their medicine, affording to buy meat for a weekend meal, or paying the gas bill. No one should have to live like that, especially families who are working hard and doing everything right. That’s why my colleagues and I wrote an Affordability Agenda focused on bringing down the everyday costs families feel the most – from groceries and health care to housing, child care, and energy. = Our plan would expand the $35 insulin cap and the $2,100 annual limit on prescription drug costs so more Americans can benefit. It extends Affordable Care Act tax credits to get premiums down and cracks down on price gouging and Wall Street greed. This is about making sure families who work hard can actually get ahead – not just scrape by. But the same special interests driving costs up are already spending big to flip this seat. They’ve already dumped over $1 million into television ads attacking me for standing up for the right thing. That’s why I need your help, friend. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = More soon, Kristen | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Kristen McDonald Rivet for Congress! Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

# [Gas vs EV: The Supercar Gods enter the arena](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4720068&post_id=190025210&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkwMDI1MjEwLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI4OTk2NTMsImV4cCI6MTc3NTQ5MTY1MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ3MjAwNjgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NjsZq48O2ujgom0JsBMkElU1nO9EfVkERJIh2_eaCTU)
### Ferrari's Electric Gamble and the Soul of the Supercar
Mar 7
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4720068&post_id=190025210&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkwMDI1MjEwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3Mjg5OTY1MywiZXhwIjoxNzc1NDkxNjUzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDcyMDA2OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0dk5E24QxFNp6oYbp8i3qL34x4TYcJSDOupIVZlTmrk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/thecapitalistmag/p/gas-vs-ev-the-supercar-gods-enter-425?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

### **Too busy to read today? Listen to The Capitalist Voice anywhere. Just click the image.**
* * *
* * *
_**" Two households, both alike in dignity, In fair Emilia-Romagna, where we lay our scene, From ancient grudge break to new mutiny, Where supercars makes civil driving licenses unclean."**_
In the summer of 1963, two men stood on opposite sides of a garage in Maranello and changed the future of speed forever. Enzo Ferrari, the austere, black-clad patriarch who had already turned his name into a synonym for victory, listened as Ferruccio Lamborghini--wealthy tractor magnate and frustrated Ferrari owner--complained that the broken clutch in his Ferrari was the same one he used in his tractors and suggested improvements.
Enzo, never one to suffer criticism lightly, reportedly replied that if Lamborghini thought he could build a better car, he should try, or words to that effect. Ferruccio did exactly that. Within months he founded Automobili Lamborghini, and the most famous rivalry in automotive history was born.
For more than six decades the prancing horse and the raging bull have defined the outer limits of what a road car can be. Their flagship machines read like a roll of automotive honor--Ferrari's 250 GTO, F40, Enzo, and LaFerrari; Lamborghini's Miura, Countach, Diablo, and Aventador--have not merely competed; they have staged a rolling opera of one-upmanship that shaped the very idea of the modern supercar. The 1966 Miura's revolutionary transverse mid-engine layout gave birth to the modern super car and forced Ferrari to respond with the Daytona. The Countach's scissor doors and angular aggression made the Ferrari 512 BB look almost pedestrian.
* * *
_**" To get some idea of what it must have been like to own a Lamborghini Countach in 1971, imagine parking stealth bomber on your lawn today" -Jeremy Clarkson**_ **[\- The Rise and Fall of the Supercar](https://substack.com/redirect/7730a5a4-8488-4fed-80a1-c710c19e8082?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* * *
When Ferrari unveiled the F40 in 1987 as the last car personally approved by Enzo, Lamborghini answered with the wild, 200-mph Diablo. The duel was never just about numbers. It was about mythology. A blood feud that was fought on the twisting hills of Northern Italy.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f3a0c3fd-b6ca-46e7-9962-782845572182?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
At the heart of that mythology lay an unspoken philosophical divide. One camp--let us call them the Progressives--believes the worth of a car is measured in lap times, aerodynamic efficiency, and raw technological supremacy. The other camp--the Purists--insists that a car without the visceral theater of sound, smell, and mechanical drama is no longer a car at all; it is a very fast appliance with all the sex appeal of a dishwasher. For decades both Ferrari and Lamborghini managed to serve both tribes. Ferrari gave the world icons like the 355 and the screaming V12 of the 812 Superfast and the hybrid LaFerrari. Lamborghini answered with the thunderous V12 Aventador and the outrageous Urus, which proved even an SUV could still feel like a bull in a china shop.
That delicate balance is now under existential strain. Lamborghini has walked away from a pure-electric future, its leadership citing "almost zero demand" in the luxury supercar segment and with true Italian flair declared its foray in to EV's "an expensive hobby." Ferrari, by contrast, is pressing ahead. Its first battery-electric vehicle, codenamed Luce, is expected to open for orders this spring. The company however has halved its 2030 EV sales target from 40 percent to 20 percent, a quiet acknowledgment perhaps that the market is not as "electrifying" as once hoped. Yet even that reduced ambition represents a historic pivot for the house of the prancing horse, but the data from the broader luxury segment is sobering.
* * *
* * *
### The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication
### Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/c7487649-4b87-4d73-bad3-21cac0858565?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
High-end electric vehicles with stellar nameplates have largely bombed. Rolls-Royce's Spectre, the first fully electric Rolls in history, has sold modestly. Bentley's Flying Spur Hybrid and Continental GT Speed Hybrid have far outsold their pure-EV siblings. Aston Martin's Lagonda project was quietly shelved. Even Porsche, the most successful electrifier among traditional sports-car makers, has seen its Taycan sales plateau while the gasoline 911 remains the undisputed profit king. The emotional connection that Lamborghini's leadership openly mourns--the howl of a V12 at 9,000 rpm, the theatrical bark on overrun, the smell of racing fuel and hot carbon--has proven much more elusive to replicate than EV evangelists predicted no matter how storied the name.
Ferrari is attempting to thread that almost impossible of needles. It must satisfy the Progressives who demand that Maranello remain at the cutting edge of technology while reassuring the Purists that the soul of the brand will survive. The company's strategy has always been to evolve without ever fully abandoning its past; the hybrid SF90 and 296 GTB showed it could blend electric torque with screaming V6 and V8 drama. But a pure EV is different. There is no engine note to synthesize, no mechanical heartbeat to preserve (and no, piping in a digital soundtrack of a wailing V8 through the stereo system is not a solution.) The risk is that in chasing the future, Ferrari alienates the very customers who have sustained the myth for ninety years.
The old rivalry, once so fertile, now feels like a referendum on two competing visions of automotive destiny. Lamborghini has chosen to double down on what it does best: theater, emotion, and unapologetic excess. Ferrari has chosen to bet that even the most devout Purists will eventually accept electrification if the car is fast enough, beautiful enough, and still (somehow?) "feels" like a Ferrari even if it has no soundtrack.
History suggests the bet is far from certain. When the Miura arrived in 1966, many traditionalists dismissed it as a toy. When the F40 appeared, some called it crude. Today we know how wrong the critics were, both cars are now revered as masterpieces that transcend time. The question is whether the electric Luce will one day be spoken of in the same breath--or whether it will be remembered as the moment the prancing horse took a turn to the glue factory?
The garage in Maranello is quiet these days. Enzo and Ferruccio are long gone. But their argument continues, now carried on not in angry words but in spreadsheets, regulatory deadlines, and the quiet hopes of engineers and enthusiasts alike. One camp still believes that progress must never come at the expense of soul. The other insists that refusing to evolve is the surest way to lose both.
**Editor 's Note:** Before you click away dear reader, we would ask you to watch what is probably one of the most moving things ever committed to film.
A glorified dishwasher just doesn't make you feel like this does.
Ferrari has a steep hill to climb.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/91f1296c-c6e9-40a9-91eb-c8c0a48fc910?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

The company's strategy has always been to evolve without ever fully abandoning its past; the hybrid SF90 and 296 GTB showed it could blend electric torque with screaming V6 and V8 drama. But a pure EV is different. There is no engine note to synthesize, no mechanical heartbeat to preserve (and no, piping in a digital soundtrack of a wailing V8 through the stereo system is not a solution.) The risk is that in chasing the future, Ferrari alienates the very customers who have sustained the myth for ninety years. The old rivalry, once so fertile, now feels like a referendum on two competing visions of automotive destiny. Lamborghini has chosen to double down on what it does best: theater, emotion, and unapologetic excess. Ferrari has chosen to bet that even the most devout Purists will eventually accept electrification if the car is fast enough, beautiful enough, and still (somehow?) "feels" like a Ferrari even if it has no soundtrack. History suggests the bet is far from certain. When the Miura arrived in 1966, many traditionalists dismissed it as a toy. When the F40 appeared, some called it crude. Today we know how wrong the critics were, both cars are now revered as masterpieces that transcend time. The question is whether the electric Luce will one day be spoken of in the same breath--or whether it will be remembered as the moment the prancing horse took a turn to the glue factory? The garage in Maranello is quiet these days. Enzo and Ferruccio are long gone. But their argument continues, now carried on not in angry words but in spreadsheets, regulatory deadlines, and the quiet hopes of engineers and enthusiasts alike. One camp still believes that progress must never come at the expense of soul. The other insists that refusing to evolve is the surest way to lose both. Editor 's Note: Before you click away dear reader, we would ask you to watch what is probably one of the most moving things ever committed to film. A glorified dishwasher just doesn't make you feel like this does. Ferrari has a steep hill to climb. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2026 The Capitalist 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

I would be honored to earn your support to become Colorado's next Democratic Secretary of State. From helping develop and implement Colorado's 2013 Voter Access & Modernized Elections Act, to passing Automatic Voter Registration in 2019, to making progressive change in Colorado for decades – I will hit the ground running to defend Colorado elections from Donald Trump.

I hope to earn your support too. Thank you for your time.

clean_body: One click. Three times the fight... | Peter, “Republicans Face Tough Battle Defending Senate Majority in 2026 Midterms” – one recent warning read. Here is what I know: If we lose the Senate, the radical Left will ram through their agenda unchecked – and God help Montana and America if that happens. As a Navy SEAL who fought for this country overseas, and now fighting for Montana in the Senate, I’m not letting that happen on my watch. That’s why I’m personally asking you to split a donation across three battleground campaigns that will determine control of the Senate in 2026.[[link]]() Ashley Moody \- Florida’s former Attorney General, who has BEATEN the Left in court time and time again. She was appointed to fill Marco Rubio’s Senate seat, and Democrats hope to flip it this November. Michael Whatley – As Chairman of the RNC in 2024, Michael Whatley helped deliver Republicans the White House, the Senate, and the House. Now, he’s fighting to win the OPEN Senate seat in North Carolina. Democrats see it as their BIGGEST chance to flip a seat. We have to win! Ashley Hinson – A congresswoman running for Iowa’s OPEN Senate Seat. After Democrats made inroads in Iowa last year, Ashley needs our urgent help. These three warriors are on the front lines defending our Republican majority – and they need reinforcements now. to their campaigns through this secure platform. It’s straightforward, efficient, and multiplies your impact. to HOLD the Senate and keep fighting for Montana values: secure borders, energy independence, wildfire solutions for our public lands, and support for our veterans.[[link]]() Tim Sheehy U.S. Senator - Montana | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." * | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Tim Sheehy is a former Navy SEAL. name: David Hogg
subject: AIPAC
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
This is why these races cost so much.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260307-1&t=1&akid=3221%2E587540%2EQwMvzw)
This is crazy, Peter:
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260307_1_aipac.q5Zg-V&refcode=em20260307-3221&t=2&akid=3221%2E587540%2EQwMvzw)
Right now, super PACs like AIPAC are flooding money into primary races to crush progressive candidates — and they’re even using shell organizations to do it.
Maybe it’s ironic to say this since I co-founded Leaders We Deserve, but here’s what I believe: **Super PACs should not exist.** My hope is that one day Leaders We Deserve won’t need to exist either. But our candidates need us behind them, or they’ll have no one boosting them when special interest groups like AIPAC try to defeat them.
If we do nothing, super PACs will flood the airwaves attacking our candidates, and these young progressive leaders won’t be equipped to respond in a real way.
_This is why these races cost so much._ It’s also why so many are cynical about politics. Money should not buy our elections — but the data show that money really does matter. That’s why it’s vital for us to help our candidates fight back – not just so we can help them win, but so that we have progressive leaders in office who will help transform our campaign finance system.
**[Peter, we need your support to ensure our young, progressive candidates have the resources they need to win — even in the face of groups like AIPAC trying to buy seats and protect the status quo. Pitch in $5 now.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260307_1_aipac.q5Zg-V&refcode=em20260307-3221&amount=5&t=3&akid=3221%2E587540%2EQwMvzw)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260307_1_aipac.q5Zg-V&amount=5&refcode=em20260307-3221&t=22&akid=3221%2E587540%2EQwMvzw)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260307-1&t=23&refcode2=3221_587540_QwMvzw&akid=3221%2E587540%2EQwMvzw)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

name: Joe Jaworski
subject: We’re going to a runoff
disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
The votes are in, and we have some news. This race for the Democratic nomination for Texas Attorney General is going to a runoff on May 26. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14193192?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=e977314a9b&e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | , The votes are in, and we have some news: **This race for the Democratic nomination for Texas Attorney General is going to a runoff on****May 26**. **First, thank you.** Thank you to every volunteer, every supporter, every student leader, every grassroots organization, and every voter who put their trust in this campaign. Over the past months we traveled across Texas together, building a coalition that believes the Office of Attorney General must serve the people, not the powerful. **Because of you, we are still standing and still fighting.** But there is another important takeaway from this result. Even with the advantages of incumbency and the Austin political establishment, my opponent **could not reach 50 percent** of the vote. That tells us something very important about this race and about the direction Texas Democrats want to go. **They want a fighter.** **They want an Attorney General who will defend voting rights, protect free speech, challenge corruption, and stand up to MAGA extremism without compromise.** That is exactly what this campaign has been about from the beginning. Now we enter the next chapter. From today until **May 26** , we will take our message to Democrats across Texas in a clear, one-on-one contest about the future of this office and the future of our party. I will continue doing what I have done throughout this campaign: traveling the state, meeting voters face to face, answering questions, and showing Texans exactly the kind of Attorney General I will be. **The road to May 26 starts today.** **Runoff elections are won by organization, momentum, and turnout. That means we will need your continued energy, your continued voice, and yes, your continued financial support so we can reach every Democratic voter in Texas before the runoff. ** [**If you believe in this campaign and the vision we are fighting for, I am asking you to stand with me again in this next phase.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=570136c615&e=5d8d80652b) Together we can win this nomination and carry our fight for justice, fairness, and democracy into the general election. Onward to May,

**
**

---

Hi team,

**Tonight, Vic Mellor is holding his extremist rally here in Rhode Island.**

He’s chosen to center his campaign around national figures tied to January 6th and Trump-style conspiracy politics. _That’s his decision._ Mine is different.

I’m spending today the same way I spend every day in this job, focused on lowering costs, protecting health care, and making sure Rhode Islanders have a representative who takes this responsibility seriously.

**This seat is not a platform for political theater.**

[That’s why my team and I set a goal to raise $6,000 before this rally at 7:30 tonight. We’re close, but not there yet. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Never lost a race.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Will you join me in the fight?

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126861995/606187911/1278937866?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.03.07_Roy-Cooper-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMjg0NTExOC00MjFhLWYxMTEtYTY5YS0wMDBkM2ExZjBhOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jsGmeu_WAJs7N4rUjUllNwYRxjFsG_c7zrqT-NMc8FI=) **Winning in a swing state isn’t easy.**

_Not when the other side is throwing everything they’ve got at you.

Not when President Trump makes you a personal target._

**That’s where things stand here in North Carolina ahead of the midterm elections.**

Our election is the most competitive U.S. Senate race anywhere in the country — it’s the one that will determine whether or not we can take back the Senate for the people and break the GOP’s one-party hold on Washington.

President Trump and his political allies are targeting me. Far-right PACs and megadonors are lined up to pour hundreds of millions into defeating me. _But I know that we can win._

You see, I’ve won some of the most competitive statewide races here in battleground NC. In fact, _I have never lost a race._ Over and over, we’ve won _together_ — and that’s how we’ll win again.

[**_Far-right Republicans are terrified that I’m the Democrat who can flip this seat from red to blue for the first time since 2008. I’m asking you to donate to help me prove that we can. clean_body: Will you join me in the fight? = Winning in a swing state isn’t easy. Not when the other side is throwing everything they’ve got at you. Not when President Trump makes you a personal target. That’s where things stand here in North Carolina ahead of the midterm elections. Our election is the most competitive U.S. Senate race anywhere in the country — it’s the one that will determine whether or not we can take back the Senate for the people and break the GOP’s one-party hold on Washington. President Trump and his political allies are targeting me. Far-right PACs and megadonors are lined up to pour hundreds of millions into defeating me. But I know that we can win. You see, I’ve won some of the most competitive statewide races here in battleground NC. In fact, I have never lost a race. Over and over, we’ve won together — and that’s how we’ll win again. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Roy Cooper and Maggie Hassan will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = Appreciate your help, Roy Cooper If you'd like to support Maggie Hassan for Senate, =. = We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak]
Friend, today is the last day of the caucus for our primary.
If you’ve committed to caucus and stepped up to become a delegate to stand with our campaign, I am incredibly grateful for you showing up. This campaign is powered by everyday Coloradans, and your willingness to take part in this grassroots process means everything.
Caucusing is where our neighbors gather, organize, and decide who will be on the ballot. It’s not about big donors or party insiders handpicking their favorite candidate.
Running a people-powered campaign across Western and Southern Colorado takes a lot of resources, whether it’s time on the road meeting voters in every corner of this district or getting the word out about our vision.
That’s why I’m asking you to give $20 or more to my campaign. Your support will help us ramp up our organizing plans and grow with confidence as the primary election heats up → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak?refcode=260307-ak-Grassroots&amount=20]
CHIP IN » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2603-q1-ak]
Whether you showed up in person or you’re supporting from home, thank you for being part of this.
— Alex Kelloff

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126862306/606189047/1674526804?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Focused_260307_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YzFmNDJlZC00MjFhLWYxMTEtYTY5YS0wMDBkM2ExZjBhOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7vmX_aF1UiFgd-dZavsFGOTUdL2X0F2xPwnNS4Xu7FE=) Derek, I know your inbox is probably flooded with emails from other campaigns right now.

So I hope you’ll understand why I also have to email you today. I’ve got to be honest, there’s many ways I’d rather spend my time than asking folks for their hard-earned dollar.

Here’s what I’m focused on right now:

Lowering the cost of groceries, energy bills, child care, and housing

Fighting for universal health care

Ensuring that innovations in AI benefit working Americans, not just big tech companies

Keeping Virginians – and all Americans – safe, and supporting our troops

Protecting our free and fair elections and standing up for democracy

But the reality is, running for office takes resources: investing in voter outreach, recruiting volunteers, opening field offices across Virginia, and so much more.

[_**I’m counting on grassroots supporters like you to help me reach my fundraising targets, so we can hit the ground running this year and stay focused on the issues that matter most. ---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=be2df09e1b&e=567664f14c)
| | | | | |
Iran's navy is at the bottom of the ocean. Everyone's covering the missiles. [Nobody's covering the oil](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=72767364b4&e=567664f14c). 87% of Iran's oil goes to China — and that changes everything about why Trump struck.
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=e67034728e&e=567664f14c)
| |
🔥 **THE IRAN STORY NOBODY IS COVERING — FOLLOW THE OIL**
87% of Iran's oil goes to China. 55% of Venezuela's does too. Trump isn't just taking out a regime — he's squeezing China's energy supply from two continents at the same time.
The US now produces more oil than Saudi Arabia and Russia combined. The Strait of Hormuz matters to China, not us. When you see the numbers side by side, the entire strategy clicks.

🚨 **FIRST TORPEDO SINKING SINCE WWII — IRAN'S NAVY IS GONE**
A US submarine sank an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka with a single torpedo. First time since 1945. The video is unreal.
Missile fire down 86%. Drones down 73%. The US just shifted from long-range strikes to flying directly overhead. Mark breaks down what that transition means — and why the military says they have plenty of munitions to finish this.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=a63279b383&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
🗳️ **TALARICO WINS TEXAS**
Mark predicted Talarico would lose. He beat Crockett 53-46% with the highest Democratic primary turnout in Texas history — 2.3 million votes. Cornyn and Paxton are headed to a May 26 runoff.
Mark owns the miss. But his breakdown of what's coming next is what matters — people of faith are about to come for Talarico's biblical claims in a way we haven't seen before. Josh Howerton's 40-minute takedown is just the start.

⚖️ **SUPREME COURT BLOCKS SCHOOLS FROM HIDING KIDS FROM PARENTS**
The Supreme Court ruled parents have a right to know. Then Vince Torres dug into school policies in Santa Fe, Albuquerque, and Las Cruces. What he found matches exactly what California just got blocked for.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=0ce3188445&e=567664f14c)
---
| |

PLUS: The NYT called Khamenei a "regional power" and Limbaugh an "attack machine" — the side-by-side will make your blood boil. And the congressional ballot is dead even at 50-50.
---
|

Friend, it's Pete Buttigieg. I want to draw your attention to a race coming up next week in Northwest Georgia that has the potential to bring a new kind of leadership for the district and help flip the U.S. House of Representatives. Shawn Harris is running for Congress in a special election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene. Shawn is a retired Army Brigadier General, a Marine veteran, and a cattle farmer who returned home after decades of service, running because he believes representation is about taking responsibility rather, not about getting attention. [**If you are able, I hope you will consider chipping in $5, $10, or whatever you can right now to help Shawn Harris win this special election. For years, this district has been defined by national drama instead of local results. Shawn is offering something steadier: leadership rooted in service, respect, and the conviction that government should work for people who are working hard and still feeling the squeeze from rising costs. That contrast was on full display late last month when Donald Trump visited the district to attempt to defend his record on affordability. The truth is families in Northwest Georgia are living a different reality: Prices remain high, household budgets are under pressure, and voters are increasingly frustrated when political rhetoric does not line up with everyday life. Shawn is running to meet people where they are and to focus on work that will lead to lower everyday costs and better results from government. He has already shown that he can compete in this district by building a coalition of Democrats, Independents, veterans, rural voters, and common-sense Republicans who are ready for serious leadership in Congress. This race matters beyond Northwest Georgia. Because it is a special election, the margins are tight and the stakes are high. A Democratic victory would narrow the gap in the House and move us closer to a majority that can protect democracy and focus on affordability rather than outrage. That outcome depends on acting now. Special elections move quickly, and organizing makes a decisive difference. That is why I am reaching out personally. The truth is families in Northwest Georgia are living a different reality: Prices remain high, household budgets are under pressure, and voters are increasingly frustrated when political rhetoric does not line up with everyday life. Shawn is running to meet people where they are and to focus on work that will lead to lower everyday costs and better results from government. He has already shown that he can compete in this district by building a coalition of Democrats, Independents, veterans, rural voters, and common-sense Republicans who are ready for serious leadership in Congress. This race matters beyond Northwest Georgia. Because it is a special election, the margins are tight and the stakes are high. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

My name is Porter Stansberry.

I’m the founder of one of the largest financial research firms in the world. Over the last 26 years we’ve helped investors navigate almost every major economic cycle.

We’ve also been on the forefront of every big financial story from the rise of Bitcoin and MRNA vaccines to robotics and artificial intelligence – just to name a few.

But today, I’m breaking the [biggest story of my career](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/u/click?_t=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_m=f55d8407244f474ba7c61d751cda3c46&_e=Acrg3Tg7pdsw1-ZatiK7pYFQLhnw3uVkNc_GbtSGHGnWsXavWZUWm0L2evZ7V572jlARdaarCb316mLAzTQBCLF-DSfd-wlBwjskFRtcKESe7Xch4BXs0qitzCFwJGcTk6qwtmBCn0AXrhc5MKOg8_mYTgD1-Z1OwfvBFO2OpH59RWtsbryp9CVbOFiZK7j4rCho3EbFU6HyaX5bk5j4LPeKpEjkv1HsM2zjeocPX_uiGF10vEVfem7Df1mhGdKRLUsZxw09tsmOODYI0qyo-z9qDG6ocCIyd_p4vQPkLUE80VRZM9BvyH7BKmXaTF8tuA_0xWg3Vv3T3nDEuB3UuijtPh6IkjnOAExkI8FupR7N5uziMqGNyqwA3-kMZw4B6E7pBYeI6dIdjAtDuT_GJhlcwKYKBV4eriu510dDvGFLMn8fDDpypLO6ssRdfeAvWoY4xvzAGyLSNkgHXvNOTsNsMWv-ihqTDdnIrI9pqMJvoFIKV3ieP6jfqV5yma3Bk7Hpy0gGWBD1PyrsUUPTWhsUERSwxOgRWdruh8owfxrfv7VrWWLSNnV1DKQkgoEL0YTuIMLGuCJ6sSe5Wqc_vlkI9L0AG6dgTDdAZpFA4W2zjX-cFpP12u9cs2VcpZMw)...

An economic story the likes of which we’ve not seen in centuries. In fact, the last – and only time – this happened was in 1776. But now, on the eve of [America’s 250th anniversary, it’s happening again](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/u/click?_t=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_m=f55d8407244f474ba7c61d751cda3c46&_e=Acrg3Tg7pdsw1-ZatiK7pYFQLhnw3uVkNc_GbtSGHGnWsXavWZUWm0L2evZ7V572jlARdaarCb316mLAzTQBCLF-DSfd-wlBwjskFRtcKESe7Xch4BXs0qitzCFwJGcTk6qwtmBCn0AXrhc5MKOg8_mYTgD1-Z1OwfvBFO2OpH59RWtsbryp9CVbOFiZK7j4rCho3EbFU6HyaX5bk5j4LDRzzesAqTX4ee2tZrPUr6CUw7wDJSSKr4-Op3G1ouBz0AeQyUS1BtNp2DXgNNH1b6AHT7hH4Gi3mhzYyWxNVOTBIdJsJD7lhOjxP9nX83YcFRYNfpm3g8Ky9aBsyG2qpbS9hM1HwOR5Ks3SdpNeSoz1pJYp3O2Ogyuq5Lqv9JxG28iF5d3CyPrR4RUQVkN9ek0YSElhE8YZO8tTFjCj4bxIeRVO5LFKUrFqC9td3fjLD_6D5O9HWjbxSa8i9nPkJmDNFKT_UPOS0enJBKyWs6cPD0TNjqBhoxr7ZryMBkmktF29_n3MOjWrO5PM5kFrZaNIozQ9UuQTQCzMqGF3OjWfO0E9tZkjFAtQ7-lZpuO7BznCFbTmezVO3nfpzYWeDNDFIXsU3OPERlEpGxHl1BB7tIHhc-_WzN4Dtr4SLD7s).

And as you’ll discover today, the aftershock of this event could “reset” not just your personal wealth, but the entire U.S. economic system:

How you work, how you vote, how you protect and build your wealth... it’s all being turned upside down by what one famous Stanford economist says is:
> _“The biggest change ever... bigger than electricity... bigger than the steam engine.”_
[Yet almost nobody is prepared for it.](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/u/click?_t=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_m=f55d8407244f474ba7c61d751cda3c46&_e=Acrg3Tg7pdsw1-ZatiK7pYFQLhnw3uVkNc_GbtSGHGnWsXavWZUWm0L2evZ7V572jlARdaarCb316mLAzTQBCLF-DSfd-wlBwjskFRtcKESe7Xch4BXs0qitzCFwJGcTk6qwtmBCn0AXrhc5MKOg8_mYTgD1-Z1OwfvBFO2OpH59RWtsbryp9CVbOFiZK7j4rCho3EbFU6HyaX5bk5j4LOp5f9sRsfgFHB1A0bh0kTm0-KdLWmiiLX6wkjDH_d4kS0ClEZfVC9dHzJ72vt2zsjHfg0_27cOiFuZvpyFrmnuJjSJvcItQ-yrYCXfWmRIVXQwYzVNZpGoSU00RcNXRtvuKCvU1WE8OfKMowwSnhT4CSd5ShVGYbb5JPaV1nTTW2sNqf_rcQfwcsDpDYGyb-wzCnAxd75qKZ-ICrpxaGNMWvOZJaorYvvA_HtPRMmMCUoE-UHn2AqlmNpWxKofxr-jJeDjmncoLA0BJdB8546pZeWDitPMTDN9ukaOuLatTXpoE_OhZJJyeI4mIIHOk_h4FN2Mt4uMi-1XJ8PmZ492wBJgw3ee5T0n62HxmXeRcdYvW4gNdguxJ9fL1Oj9iA2L8BvvA_YjArES6E0ShJvcb19-kXFfUJ7d5quFQDxZ3) So, if you’ve been watching the chaos of the past year unfold, struggling to understand what it all means... you’re about to get many - if not all - of the answers you’ve been searching for.

And, most importantly, what it all means for you, your money, and your investment portfolio in the months ahead

Because as you’ll discover, everything from the government taking stakes in companies like Intel, Lithium Americas, and MP Materials.

To Trump’s strike on Venezuela... his deal with Greenland... his seemingly never-ending slew of executive orders... and increasingly centralized grip over the economy...

All the way to the surging popularity of radical socialist politicians like Bernie Sanders, AOC, and Zohran Mamdani...

It’s all deeply and inexorably intertwined in what is, without a doubt, the most consequential story of the year.

clean_body: I’m the founder of one of the largest financial research firms in the world. Over the last 26 years we’ve helped investors navigate almost every major economic cycle. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | My name is Porter Stansberry. Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

They need to hear from us now. [**Add your name to remind Congress that the American people do not want this war. clean_body: | | | Friend, While the War Powers Resolution votes in both the House and Senate were extremely close, we failed to stop Trump’s illegal war on Iran that is quickly becoming a regional war. But there is still something we can do to stop the killing of innocent civilians. Trump is requesting $50 billion for his ongoing war and we’re hearing that even Democrats who voted against authorizing the war could still vote to fund it. They need to hear from us now. | Remember, this authoritarian administration has taken healthcare and nutrition benefits away from tens of millions of people to pay for his tax cuts for billionaires. He and Republicans have handed hundreds of billions of dollars to ICE, Border Patrol, and DHS to carry out their terror campaign. They passed a nearly $1 trillion Pentagon budget―the largest in history. We need to make sure all members come together and reject funding for Trump’s illegal war on Iran. Americans want investments in our communities, not endless war abroad. Trump is acting on the violent fantasies of the American political elite and the Israeli apartheid government, ignoring the vast majority of Americans who say loud and clear: No More Wars. Together, we will not be silent in the face of violent authoritarian overreach. In solidarity, Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . name: Must Read
subject: Toxic Water Warning
body:
Hometown water supply in danger
---
| | | | | |
---
| | | | | [View in browser](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D "View in Browser")
---
_**A Special Message from our Sponsor**_ _Please note that views expressed in this message do not necessarily reflect those of_ _Parler_ _and do not constitute an endorsement or recommendation by_ _Parler_ _._
---
| Toxic water warning | **The Invisible Threat In Millions of Homes** [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **The worst drinking water outbreak in U.S. history sent 403,000 to the hospital and killed 69. No alarms. No warnings.** The drinking water in their homes appeared safe, but was contaminated with a deadly pathogen. Families didn't fail. The systems they trusted failed. This outbreak proved that clear, normal-looking water can be dangerously contaminated. That's why experts still study this outbreak for one reason: It can happen again. In fact, here's what should truly concern you... Federal authorities and national security experts are now reporting something even more disturbing: **The FBI warns the next deadly contamination may be _intentional_.** _Hackers are attacking water systems in our home towns, overriding safety protocols, weaponizing our utilities against us and positioning themselves to strike when the signal comes._ America's enemies have found a way to strike without firing a single shot, and national security experts warn it's not just China. Iran, Russia and North Korea are also preparing coordinated attacks. Overflowing, damaging and poisoning the lifegiving systems we trust to keep us safe. **Like it or not, your local tap water may be our country's weakest link.** Deteriorating infrastructure. Systems years beyond their expiration dates. Negligent corporations. And God-knows-what in the water we drink. We're up against arsenic, plastics, chlorine, forever chemicals, hormone-altering drug residue and a host of other contaminants. Listen, I'm not trying to be the messenger of doom... _**But protecting the water supply in your home is more important than ever.**_ If you don't take steps to be prepared, you and your family could end up vulnerable. Forced to choose between drinking poisoned, toxic or dirty water, or going without water altogether. It sounds harsh... But the truth is that even good people with good intentions can make serious mistakes. Mistakes like...
* **Getting filters that claim to purify your water, but don't prove it.**
* If a company doesn't have certifications from a third-party lab, show lab tests or reveal how many gallons were tested... that's a bad sign. After a couple gallons, most water filters on the market stop working for scary contaminants and start passing them into your water.
* **Getting bottled water with a 2-year shelf life and risking plastic leaching into your supply.**
* Not to mention all the money you'll waste, and the work required to carry cases of water. Plus with bottled water, you never really know what's been filtered out.
* **Or simply using a pitcher in the fridge... with slapshot filtration power and limited capacity.**
* Most pitchers hold a couple cups of water at a time. Not nearly enough to drink, cook and clean in an emergency. You'll run out in no time, just when your life depends on it.

Well we have great news to share! Folks have been calling, emailing and leaving Facebook messages asking about our just-released... **NEW AND IMPROVED...** [**NEW Ultra-Fast MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System!**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **5X Faster Flow Rate for Perfect, Delicious Drinking Water** [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **But hurry - we have limited stock, and this MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System is first-come first-served. We only have a small number of units to go around.** Order today you'll also get a FREE Bonus Package with 7 FREE Gifts worth $263.75!
* **FREE Gift #1:** FREE Aqua-Tote Collapsible Water Container ($16.95 Value)
* **FREE Gift #2:** FREE Personal Water Filter Straw ($24.95 Value)
* **FREE Gift #3:** FREE "Water Solutions" Online Class ($149.00 Value)
* **FREE Gift #4:** FREE Water Survival Digital Guide ($9.95 Value)
* **FREE Gift #5:** FREE "What's in your Tap Water" Digital Guide ($9.95 Value)
* **FREE Gift #6:** $25.00 Savings Certificate
* **FREE Gift #7:** FREE Shipping and Handling

[**Get Your MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System Rushed To You At This Special Pricing Plus All Your FREE Bonuses While Supplies Last!**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) The best part is... _**This advanced filtration technology filters the nastiest, filthiest, bacteria-riddled and parasite infested water using no chemicals, no electricity and no moving parts!**_ It's powered by the same technology that the U.S. Military and NASA trust to filter their water. But now available for you in your home. You're among the first to get access to these advanced, carbon-block water filters designed to filter water on your countertop at a sub-micron level without power or water hookup. **So you can reduce...** Harmful Bacteria - up to 99.9999%
Life-Threatening Viruses - up to 99.99%
Disease-Carrying Cysts - up to 99.9% Plus, this table-top unit targets 200+ dangerous contaminants from your tap water, including bacteria, viruses, toxic chemicals, arsenic, heavy metals, pharmaceuticals, VOCs and more. All in the same, best-selling system thousands of Americans trust in their homes. [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **The NEW & improved Filtration System may look the same...** But on the inside, these proprietary filters pack an even bigger punch than before. They're MORE reliable and easier to replace, too. So you get safer, cleaner water for you and your family. **But we have very limited stock, and we've been sold out of this filtration powerhouse for a while.** In fact, it's been a very long while. Months and months, actually. [**Now we've just got a fresh batch in!**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) And if you need to take it with you? No problem. You're not tied to it like a whole-house filtration system. That kind of thing won't do you any good if you need to bug out. **And YES there's an interest-FREE payment plan!** Yes, right now, you can get your MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System on a convenient, no-credit-check, interest-FREE payment plan. We know you're good for it. You'll have the **peace of mind** knowing that you're doing everything you can to keep your family safer, by securing your MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System today for fresher, cleaner water for years to come. [**Get Your MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System Rushed To You At This Special Pricing Plus All Your FREE Bonuses While Supplies Last!**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **Stay Safe. Stay Prepared.**

Frank Bates
Patriot Headquarters [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) P.S. Thanks to your support, we're able to provide support to important charities that help American veterans and their families. Charities like Fisher House, A Soldier's Child Foundation, Team Rubicon and Operation Homefront. Derrick loves the convenience of his MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System: _"It was so easy to install. Within minutes we had water that tasted so good, we couldn't believe it. We don't like the chlorine in tap water and we got tired of lugging around cases of bottled water."_ **Customers Love Their Ultimate
Water Filtration System!** [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **_Cyndi L._**_
"No question... beats the competition. After reviewing several other companies and seeing that I was not the only one thinking this way, as everyone was sold out of products, I decided to go with 4Patriots. They seem to be good people who are very helpful and their products seemed above average to others."_ _**Michael B.**
"Saves me a ton of money and grocery-store headaches. During the pandemic, water wasn't always available. Not so with the Purifier. We love it and I have 5 gallons of water on hand all the time."_ _**Faith P.**
"Water you can actually trust. This is so much better than storing gallons and gallons of bottled water. This enables us to make existing, natural water sources drinkable. I feel really good knowing that in an emergency situation, we'd be able to filter our water."_ _**Carol B.**
"Stop worrying about what's in the water! We use our system for drinking and cooking but you can use it almost anywhere. It's easy to use and it doesn't take long to filter. The water tastes pure and delicious."_ _**Rick O.**
"Easiest way to upgrade your drinking water. We chose this one because it handles everything and it's affordable. You never know when things will go out on you."_ _**Thomas P.**
"Finally, I don't have to keep refilling my pitcher all day. I'm very happy with my water filter. Not only does our water now taste good (it tasted dirty from the tap) but the large capacity tank means I'm not refilling small pitchers repeatedly during the day."_ [**Get Your MegaFlow Ultimate Water Filtration System Rushed To You At This Special Pricing Plus All Your FREE Bonuses While Supplies Last!**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)

name: Jane Fonda
subject: I want to show you something.

body:
This is exactly why we do this work.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126863267/606191712/1328794144?refcode=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&ms=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU4YmY0Yzk0LTQ2MWEtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTFmMGE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CEiTZiYg8wuZxo4pSwmOe7JA4UikcsH7eM1ppqq5_ck=&emci=d9bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&emdi=e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&ceid=4823105)
---
Friends, I started the Jane Fonda Climate PAC to elect the next generation of leaders who would stand up to Big Oil and the autocratic forces that put profit over people and our planet. Trump’s unconstitutional war in Iran is a reminder that the leaders we need aren’t just fighting for climate justice. They’re standing up to the same greed and unchecked power that threatens our democracy. **Some of the bravest voices in the room have been our own JanePAC-endorsed candidates and elected officials. I want you to see their words for yourself:** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126863268/606191713/2105508926?refcode=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&ms=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU4YmY0Yzk0LTQ2MWEtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTFmMGE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CEiTZiYg8wuZxo4pSwmOe7JA4UikcsH7eM1ppqq5_ck=) _These are the leaders we're proud to support._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126863269/606191714/2105508926?refcode=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&ms=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU4YmY0Yzk0LTQ2MWEtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTFmMGE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CEiTZiYg8wuZxo4pSwmOe7JA4UikcsH7eM1ppqq5_ck=) _People who refuse to hedge, stay silent, or look the other way._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/126863270/606191715/2105508926?refcode=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&ms=ea.e8bf4c94-461a-f111-a69a-000d3a1f0a97&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU4YmY0Yzk0LTQ2MWEtZjExMS1hNjlhLTAwMGQzYTFmMGE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CEiTZiYg8wuZxo4pSwmOe7JA4UikcsH7eM1ppqq5_ck=) _People who are willing to stand up and say no when an authoritarian administration tries to drag this country into yet another forever war._ We need more leaders like these — in Congress, in statehouses, and in attorneys general offices — who will speak truth to power even when it’s uncomfortable. _And_ we need to ramp up our resources _now_ to compete with the oily billionaires and the misinformation machine working to buy these seats in November. **[That’s why I’m reaching out to you directly. clean_body: This is exactly why we do this work. | Friends, I started the Jane Fonda Climate PAC to elect the next generation of leaders who would stand up to Big Oil and the autocratic forces that put profit over people and our planet. Trump’s unconstitutional war in Iran is a reminder that the leaders we need aren’t just fighting for climate justice. They’re standing up to the same greed and unchecked power that threatens our democracy. Some of the bravest voices in the room have been our own JanePAC-endorsed candidates and elected officials. I want you to see their words for yourself: = These are the leaders we're proud to support. = People who refuse to hedge, stay silent, or look the other way. = People who are willing to stand up and say no when an authoritarian administration tries to drag this country into yet another forever war. We need more leaders like these — in Congress, in statehouses, and in attorneys general offices — who will speak truth to power even when it’s uncomfortable. And we need to ramp up our resources now to compete with the oily billionaires and the misinformation machine working to buy these seats in November. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = In solidarity, xx Jane = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. name: Team Hayes
subject: The GOP is taking notice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| | Team, Democrats have momentum. In the year since Trump was elected, Democrats have won several key races — in some cases beating out Trump’s win in historically red districts by double digits. The GOP is taking notice and readying themselves for November’s election by bringing in HUGE fundraising hauls — money that will go towards protecting their vulnerable politicians and trying to flip competitive seats like Jahana’s. We need to respond in kind. We need to raise the resources necessary to keep strong Democrats, like Jahana, in office, so we can take real action to stop Donald Trump. [_**We have the momentum now. Let us use it to bring in the funds we need to flip the House. body:
The work continues |
#

Team, When Gretchen Whitmer talks about what comes next for Michigan, she’s focused on a few things: 📚 Making sure kids learn to read.
👷♀️ Building more homes people can actually afford.
🏥 Lowering the cost of health care. It’s the kind of work that makes a real difference for families trying to get by and get ahead. Michigan has already made real progress thanks to Governor Whitmer — from cancelling medical debt for hundreds of thousands of people to expanding free pre-K and school meals. Now, the work continues. **[Fight Like Hell PAC is helping support leaders like Governor Whitmer who are focused on getting things done. Folks, it’s Sarah, Christina’s campaign manager.
We are 150 days away from the August Primary Election.
Polls have already shown that Christina is the ONLY Democrat in the field who has the background and experience we need to beat the presumptive Trump wannabe this November—now, we need the resources to get that message to every single voter in the next 150 days.
As a prosecutor, Christina knows how to hold the powerful accountable—and that’s exactly what we’ll do. Instead of catering to the rich and powerful, we’ll fight for working families here in MI-10—especially for lifelines like SNAP, Medicare, and Social Security that families depend on to survive.
Every day counts. Every dollar counts. Even $1.50, $10.50, or $150 helps us keep our campaign strong, fierce, and unstoppable. Will you chip in today to make sure we get there? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260307_150days_act&amount=10.50]

Sarah

name: Katie Porter
subject: Running after my dog and for Californians
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40947%2E3422442%2E4aXA58)
[](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260307_1_ak.JfGuFv&refcode=em20260307-40947&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40947%2E3422442%2E4aXA58)
Peter,
I love running. Not just after my adorable dog Poppy, but to fight for working families in this governor’s campaign.
But right now, our race is heating up—and every day until the primary counts for getting our message out on TV, on people’s phones, and face to face with voters.
This race is between us and two MAGA Republicans ready to bow down to Trump. If nothing changes in the polls, there's a serious chance there won’t be a Democrat on November’s ballot.
Your support helps me stay on the campaign trail to make sure that doesn’t happen. My team has a plan to grow our lead by reaching voters across the state, and we need your help to make it happen.
**[Can you chip in $5 or any amount to help me beat two MAGA Republicans and fight for working Californians?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260307_1_ak.JfGuFv&refcode=em20260307-40947&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40947%2E3422442%2E4aXA58)**
